seoTitle: How to Create a New Ticket in Zendesk Manually
metaDescription: Learn how to log a fresh Zendesk support ticket manually for phone calls or offline requests, from setting the requester to submitting the final status.
intro: Not every support issue arrives as an inbound email, and phone calls or in-person requests still need to be documented. This guide covers manually opening a new ticket in Zendesk, assigning the right requester and owner, and submitting it with the correct status.

steps.heading: Open a new ticket tab
steps.body: Click the "+ Add" tab control in the top navigation bar to start a fresh ticket.

steps.heading: Set the requester
steps.body: Search your existing contact list or register a new customer by entering their name or email in the requester field.

steps.heading: Assign ownership
steps.body: Set the group and individual assignee fields to route the ticket to the right team or agent.

steps.heading: Write the subject and details
steps.body: Compose a clear subject line and describe the issue in the ticket body.

steps.heading: Set ticket properties
steps.body: Adjust dropdowns for Type, Priority, and Brand so the ticket is properly categorized.

steps.heading: Submit the ticket
steps.body: Choose a status such as New, Open, or Pending from the dropdown and submit to add it to your active queue.

faq.question: Can I create a ticket for a customer who doesn't exist in the system yet?
faq.answer: Yes, you can register a new requester directly from the ticket creation screen if the customer doesn't already have a profile.

faq.question: What status should I use for a manually created ticket?
faq.answer: It depends on your workflow, but New is common for a fresh unassigned issue, while Open or Pending fit tickets already being worked.

faq.question: Can I attach files to a manually created ticket?
faq.answer: Yes, the ticket composer supports file attachments just like a standard reply.

faq.question: Is there a keyboard shortcut for creating a new ticket?
faq.answer: Zendesk supports keyboard shortcuts for common actions, so check your agent settings for the current shortcut to open a new ticket tab.
